finding_results.notes: Roughened the ridge decision scene (lines 141-161). Replaced polished punchlines and parallel-echo exchanges with pressure-damaged speech. Three turns now show interruption or unfinished syntax: Kem's self-correcting relay of Osen's information (line 141: 'She said — the Council sent someone'), Vel's aborted sentence about the door (line 145: 'I spent eight years walking toward a door they — eight years'), and Vel's abandoned catalogue of outcomes (line 153: 'Maybe it holds, maybe it — I can't tell you what the numbers are anymore'). Removed the crafted parallel exchange ('That sounds like the Hoarded' / 'It sounds like me being late'), the narrator-admiring-dialogue line ('It was the closest she had come all journey to saying impossible'), the quotable thesis line ('It's the only one that reaches the door'), and the polished banter closing ('Good. I prefer plans with measurable optimism' / 'You've had six weeks to fix that preference'). Replaced Vel's punchline response to 'That's a terrible plan' with flat 'Yeah.' Functional outcomes preserved: ruined math conveyed, tunnel plan established, risk acknowledged, two-hour timing with light angle set. Character voices maintained: Vel quieter and more clipped under pressure, Kem talking more but stumbling through the relay.

summary: Single finding addressed: roughened the ridge decision scene after Osen reveals the threshold degradation. Replaced composed, quotable dialogue turns with pressure-fractured speech showing self-correction, unfinished syntax, and abandoned thoughts. Cut narrator commentary that admired the dialogue and polished banter that resolved the scene too neatly. All functional outcomes (ruined math, tunnel plan, risk assessment, timing) preserved. Net compression of approximately 10 lines from removing redundant punchline exchanges.
